claims.claim: For LiNi0.9Co0.05Mn0.05O2 batteries (Sample A, 211 Ah, 342.06 Wh/kg), thermal runaway was triggered when the positive tab reached 119°C and the negative tab reached 123°C, with a critical temperature defined as a temperature change rate of 1°C/s.
claims.quote: The critical temperature for TR is defined as a temperature change rate of 1 °C/s. The temperature of the positive tab rose to 119 °C, and the negative tab rose to 123 °C.

claims.claim: Sample A (211 Ah, 342.06 Wh/kg) reached a peak chamber temperature of 557.5°C and a peak chamber pressure of 386.5 kPa during thermal runaway, while Sample B (256 Ah, 325.82 Wh/kg) reached a peak chamber temperature of 409.86°C and a peak chamber pressure of 330.5 kPa.
claims.quote: The highest temperature inside the chamber reached 557.5 °C, and the highest pressure inside the chamber reached 386.5 kPa. For Sample B... The highest temperature inside the chamber reached 409.86 °C, and the highest pressure inside the chamber was 330.5 kPa.

claims.claim: The higher-energy-density Sample A (342.06 Wh/kg) exhibited a mass loss rate of 73% during thermal runaway, while the lower-energy-density Sample B (325.82 Wh/kg) exhibited a mass loss rate of 64.87%, indicating that higher energy density leads to more severe mass loss.
claims.quote: Sample A showed a larger mass loss after TR, indicating a more severe TR phenomenon... The mass loss rates for A and B were 73% and 64.87%

claims.claim: The normalized gas production of Sample B (256 Ah) was 2.182 L/Ah compared to 1.958 L/Ah for Sample A (211 Ah) at standard conditions (0°C, 101 kPa), indicating that gas production increases with battery capacity.
claims.quote: the gas production is converted to standard conditions, resulting in 1.958 L/Ah for Sample A and 2.182 L/Ah for Sample B. It can be observed that gas production is related to the capacity of the battery.

claims.claim: The mixed vent gases from Sample A (211 Ah) had a lower flammable limit of 8.49% and a flammable range of 49.81%, while Sample B (256 Ah) had a lower flammable limit of 7.43% and a flammable range of 44.45%, indicating Sample B gas poses a higher ignition risk due to its lower LFL.
claims.quote: Table 6. The flammable limits. Sample UFL LFL Flammable Range A 58.3 8.49 49.81 B 51.88 7.43 44.45

claims.claim: The linearized gas flow rate during thermal runaway for the high-power 18650 cell (SonyVTC5A) is 22.15 gs−1 and for the high-energy cell (LGMJ1) is 27.92 gs−1.
claims.quote: Our results show, that the linearized gas flow rate for the high-power and high-energy cell is 22.15 gs−1 and 27.92 gs−1, respectively.

claims.claim: The peak gas velocity during thermal runaway for the high-energy 18650 cell (LGMJ1, NMC811) follows a single peak asymmetrical pattern with a maximum of 398.5 ms−1, while the high-power cell (SonyVTC5A, NCA) shows a bumpy pattern with a maximum of 260.9 ms−1.
claims.quote: in case of the high-energy cell, it follows a single peak asymmetrical pattern with a peak of 398.5 ms−1, while the high-power cell shows a bumpy pattern with a maximum gas velocity of 260.9 ms−1.

claims.claim: The transition time from safety valve opening to thermal runaway for the high-power NCA cell (SonyVTC5A) was 69.46 ± 12.67 s, whereas the non-reheated high-energy NMC811 cell (LGMJ1) required 189.54 ± 87.56 s, meaning the high-energy cell took at least twice as long.
claims.quote: It took 69.46 ± 12.67 s for the SonyVTC5A cells to go from opening of the safety valve into TR, whereas the non-reheated LGMJ1 cells needed 189.54 ± 87.56 s.

claims.claim: The averaged mass loss during the thermal runaway stage was 57.50% for SonyVTC5A and 63.45% for LGMJ1, as measured by the oscilloscope.
claims.quote: The boxplots (e) and (f) underneath show averaged mass losses with the Osci for SonyVTC5A and LGMJ1 of 57.50% and 63.45%, respectively.

claims.claim: The gas flow rate and gas velocity during venting can be determined from only the recoil force and weight force measurements, without requiring knowledge of vent pressure, vent temperature, vent area size, or ejecta composition.
claims.quote: With this approach, the size and stability of venting area, venting temperature and cell temperature, venting pressure and cell pressure, as well as the composition of the ejecta are irrelevant for deriving the gas velocity and gas flow rate.

claims.claim: The simulated gas flow rates from venting and thermal runaway propagation models for 18650 cells ranged from 2.5 gs−1 to 3.5 gs−1, which is one order of magnitude lower than the experimentally measured values of 22.15 gs−1 and 27.92 gs−1.
claims.quote: the simulated gas flow rates are given with 2.5 gs−1 [31] to 3.5 gs−1 [35] and one order of magnitude lower than our measured gas flow distribution with 22.15 ± 14.70 gs−1 for SonyVTC5A and 27.92 ± 9.500 gs−1 for LGMJ1 cells.

claims.claim: The expansion force of a 58 Ah Li(Ni0.8Co0.1Mn0.1)O2 prismatic battery reached a peak value of 1980 N at −13.5 s relative to voltage-zero, representing a 12-fold increase within a duration of 2.5 s starting from −15 s.
claims.quote: At the −15 s mark: Expansion force initiates a sharp ascent, reaching a peak value of 1980 N at −13.5 s, representing a 12-fold increase within a duration of 2.5 s.

claims.claim: During thermal runaway of a 58 Ah Li(Ni0.8Co0.1Mn0.1)O2 battery, the temperature measured 1 cm above the safety release valve (TE) exhibited a maximum rate of temperature rise of 93 °C/s within the time frame of −13 s to −5 s relative to voltage-zero.
claims.quote: within the −13 s to −5 s timeframe, TE exhibits the highest rate of temperature rise, reaching 93 °C/s

claims.claim: During thermal runaway of a 58 Ah Li(Ni0.8Co0.1Mn0.1)O2 battery, the maximum momentary eruption velocity upon safety valve opening was 90 m/s, occurring at the 1.5-s mark, with solid particles jetting to heights of up to 3.4 m.
claims.quote: The most intense occurrence of the first mode transpires at the 1.5-s mark, with a momentary eruption velocity of 90 m/s upon the opening of the safety valve... the generated solid particles can jet to heights of up to 3.4 m.

claims.claim: Explosive phenomena during thermal runaway were initiated by ejected sparks igniting combustible gases expelled during the jetting process, with the explosion center located within 0.1 to 0.5 m from the safety valve.
claims.quote: explosive phenomena are initiated by the ignition of the ejected sparks, which ignite the combustible gases expelled during the jetting process... the explosion center to be within a range of 0.1 to 0.4 m from the safety valve.

claims.claim: During thermal runaway of a 58 Ah Li(Ni0.8Co0.1Mn0.1)O2 battery, the mass loss rate was 32.4 ± 2%, the maximum flame propagation velocity exceeded 17 m/s, and the maximum flame area reached 0.79 m².
claims.quote: The mass loss rate (%) 32.4 ± 2, The maximum flame propagation velocity (m/s) >17, The maximum flame area (m2) 0.79
